_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about weather and seasons, including the seasonal calendars used by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples.

_source.w: We are learning about weather and seasons, including the seasonal calendars used by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples.
y: Understanding weather and seasons helps us know what to expect and how to prepare.
Aboriginal seasonal calendars teach us that there are many ways to read the land and sky.
Learning about seasons helps us notice patterns in nature all around us.
eg: Keeping a class weather chart and recording if it is sunny, rainy, or windy each day.
Comparing the four Western seasons with the six or more seasons in an Aboriginal seasonal calendar.
Noticing that certain animals or plants appear at different times of year.
Learning that Wurundjeri People know it is a certain season when the wattle blooms.
Talking about how weather affects what we wear and what we do outside.
Looking at how weather is different in another part of Australia compared to where we live.
